definition: EV adoption has passed the S-curve inflection point at ~18-20% global new sales share (2024). Historical base rate: 8/10 comparable technologies doubled penetration within 5-7 years of crossing 10%; 0/20 technologies at 15%+ reversed to single digits. 30%+ global share by 2030 is the base case at ~65-70% probability.
evidence: 8/10 technologies doubled penetration within 5-7 years of crossing 10%
0/20 technologies at 15%+ reversed to single digits
Infrastructure-dependent transitions follow 15-30 year tails from 10% to 50% share
12-15 year average vehicle lifespan constrains fleet turnover
open_questions: Will global EV sales share reach 35% by end of 2028 (smartphone curve test)?
Does charging infrastructure become a binding constraint in developing markets?
At what EV share do ICE vehicle fixed costs create a profitability tipping point (estimated 30-40%)?
